content: Is there a good team for d tide centered around Wanda?
ah yes, this argument is very valid
it makes a lot of sense and i definitely agree with it
one of the best carries in the game wants to use good supports because it makes them even better
that's so true
this is exactly why ghelot is trash really, she wants to use thais
tawil is F tier character, she hoards hameln
man every time i play a strong carry and i put horla on the team i'm just like, ugh imagine hoarding a good support just so you can hard carry a super difficult a5, this is such a terrible character
it's almost like supports exist to support and supporting a strong carry gives you better returns than supporting a bad one
Yeah...Flares is delusional <:dead:1356082627509092372>
it's just the veteran mindset
they have invested characters and go "well i wouldn't pull on this new character because I have an invested one, therefore nobody should"
if you have half a dozen fully built dps and you're short on supports for dtide, yeah it doesn't really do much for you to pull for another one and spend resources on them
meanwhile a new player has neither ghelot nor pollux, but has thais

should they pull one dps that's more difficult to play and has a lot of conditionals and gets countered by multiple story fights, or should they pull the new dps that works with the same team/supports and is easy to play and clears all content in the game
clearly the answer is the old one!!! the vet has it and they're doing fine so nobody should ever pull the new one

But he is recommending stuff based on his beliefs that it's a must and a best way to invest for a newcomer. Iirc he was mentioning that he cleared the stuff on a new acc or smth and that's why he sees both sides of the coin. After that statement he is either delusional or just trolling all of his viewership.
I don't know of any alt accounts that flares has
but i have half a dozen screenshots of my alt account clears and i can point to multiple new players in this channel alone who progressed a2 story in a span of weeks using castor pollux

it's funny to me seeing all the arguments about team comps and building stuff for d-tide meanwhile i'm just vibing in my corner going "i keep pollux and castor on a team together because i don't want them to be separated :-)"
like. on one hand i get the desire to have good builds for d-tide but also, it feels like a good way to stress yourself out and make the game less fun to play
(also i'm someone who sped through a lot of arc 1 with said team of castor and pollux so maybe my opinion isn't really valid, but i'm offering my 2 cents regardless)
a lot of discussion is also just...focused entirely too much on dtide seeing as a ton of players don't really care about it nor do they really interface with it on a significant (390+) level
Maybe I thought like that because of this statement, not sure. Either way, him pushing bluewill, not recommending ultra, having questionable tierlist and stuff....
Yeah
i check follower profiles when i have people to add ingame and honestly like 90% of the people i see ingame are like max 350 dtide despite usually being in a2 ch3/4

yeah like. . . d-tide wants lots of teams but i feel like you don't *have* to have The Most Optimal Teams as long as you can still do what you wanna do in d-tide, assuming you *do* wanna do d-tide, yknow?
it's a surprisingly small subset of the population
which isn't to say that it isn't good content and that dtide strategy isn't important

i havent played since uh... gdoll i think, or corposant, whichever came later. Which characters are good now? (and which became mid?)
i havent played that much after realm merging got added btw
it's just that if people want dtide advice, they're going to ask for dtide advice, and telling people specifically to build around a game mode they might never seriously interface with is...I dunno
not really optimal from a new player guidance perspective imo
nods nods
pretty much all of the new limited (so everyone from gdoll forwards) are good in some form
a lot of people consider gdoll herself somewhat mid now
but if you've already pulled/invested into her she still works fine
kath, corpo, clem, pollux are all amazingly strong characters that can be used pretty much everywhere
dore is solid for low investment/f2p accounts and can do story, but runs into some scaling issues in super endgame content
pickman is an amazing support but slightly more niche in team usage compared to the generalist behemoths
am i missing anyone....
Just pull all the new units

castor is very good for shield/support but can also be good dps on his own (especially if you've got someone to proc his corrosion)
i. .  don't have anything to say really about the others bc i've only played castor and pollux ahaha ;w;
oh yeah corpo is an absolute storymode behemoth but is weaker than the other a2 limiteds in dtide
Surely everything will work out with power creep
i see, thanks for the help
People are playing a lot more caro ultra these days if you missed out on that because of the realm merging. Try it out, its fun :).
if you didn't already have thais, definitely pull for her
on top of being near-universally considered the strongest support in the game, caro mix is one of the best qol realm mixes now
yeah thais, is great, ive been running caro before the merging
chaos (tawil), caro (uvhash because hes cooler than ghelot) and aequor (aurita is fun)
never touched ultra, maybe i will one day
realm merging :?

as a side note, out of curiosity, what *does* one recommend to a new(er) player who wants to do well in d-tide? are there any general tips one recommends, or is it based on your specific team comps only?
what's Miryam's best covenant?

specific dtide advice in terms of what teams to run will necessarily revolve around your box, but if you mean general pulling advice the easiest way to really sum it up is pull for a2 limited characters
because pretty much all of them are current meta powerhouses and have some way to build around

so you're at 3 dtide teams and just need to find components for 2 more
hello^^ I'm pretty new at this game, and I can't help but be confused about covenants;; may I know what the recommended main and sub stats are for Pollux?
is it a bad idea to keep the bird twins on the same team for d-tide?
gmurphy is certainly one of them, kath can be too (wants e3 though)
it's not a bad idea, but it is a matter of investment
both of the birds are strong enough to solo carry teams in dtide so if you put them together, you're wasting the opportunity cost of an extra clear
also, is it *necessary* to get 390+ in d-tide? i've seen lots of people talk about how important it is but i still don't entirely understand *how* it's important?
aah i see i see
if you have the roster to get clears regardless, it's fine
but most people generally don't
pollux basically scales only off of his character level and crit stats and nothing else, so you want pure crit on his covenants
entirely critrate/critdmg (how much critrate specifically depends on your wheels and how much he has to begin with)
no, realistically you get access to everything at 350
you get marginally more rewards with every 5 point increment
I see, thank you! :)) <33
getting to 375 will get you another set of materials for gnosis primers, and getting to 400 past that will get you another set of materials for refined gnosis primers
so someone that does 350 every dtide will get gnosis/refined gnosis at a slower rate than someone who gets 400 every dtide
i see i see
that said, both still gain those mats, it's just less of them
so it's a question of do you want to be able to level 80 a character once every 4 months, or once every 6 months
previously you couldn't mix realms except chaos + other realm. I think around start of arc 2 they started allowing this?
(numbers not accurate, idr the actual numbers i'm just making them up)
oh
isn't there like. . . don't you have to get a character to +12 to be able to level them past a certain point? is that level 80 or level 70?
what's the best realm to merge with ultra?
is that why chaos doesnt nerf other realm now?

level 60 is the base level cap
gnosis primers can get to level 70
refined gnosis primers can get to level 80
+8 on a character gives +5 lvl cap, meaning you can hit level 85
+12 on a character gives another +5 lvl cap, meaning you can hit level 90
level 90 is the absolute maximum, which is only reachable with a +12
aah ok ok, thank you!
level 80 is doable on anyone even without dupes
all realm mixes are good now, which one you prefer generally revolves around your choice of dps/supports
ultra has some of the best supports in the game so it's more like ultra gets mixed into dps carries to support whatever realm they are
ghelot's standard comp has been ultra mix ever since realm mixing pretty much, since horla/clem
Nvm lens too fast lol

you do also get 1 for each hard mode a2 story chapter clear you do

+prototype

The **Prototype Horizon** is one of the most valuable items in the game. It is a limited character duplicate selector, allowing you to choose a free duplicate (Enlightenment level) for any limited SSR Awakener character you already own. 

You can purchase this  monthly from the in-game "Sediment Exchange" Store using a currency called Sediment, which is primarily obtained from doing gacha pulls.
+pyramid

This item is called **Origin Of Time**, it provides two main benefits:

**Full Refund**: Recovers all XP and materials you invested in the character you use it on.

**Duplicate Conversion**: Only for Standard Awakeners (not for Limited in the Standard pool or the starting SR Awakeners), converts all additional copies into Standard "selectors," which can subsequently be used to obtain or duplicate other Standard Awakeners of your choice (not Limited in Standard).

This process is also applicable to **Standard Wheels** (except the Limited in the Standard pool).

It is primarily obtained from the developers through special in-game mail during specific events, such as anniversaries or major updates. It is used for re-allocating resources to build a better team.
+store

This guide gives a general priority overview of everything you can buy in the game store (not with real currency).

[Store Guide](https://discordapp.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1442292634575175710)

Hi everyone, new player here. I’ve been researching on what to pull and have been using my standard pulls for the blue banner. Apart from that, any of the limited banners are good to pull on at the moment?

Fauxborn Banner:
1 Copy is a Keyflare(Support) Awakener. 3-4 Copies(including base copy) is a DPS Awakener. Solid and good as Keyflare and DPS Awakener. One of the Best DPS in the game.

Thais Banner:
1 Copy as a solid Keyflare/Aliemus Support Awakener. 3 Copies(including base copy), makes her become a broken Support Awakener. One of the Best Supports in the game.

Oh, and try to grab 1 copy of the WoD for both Fauxborn and Thais. Fauxborn’s Sig WoD is Handsize which makes it automatically a must pull. Thais WoD is Handsize and probably the most broken(or useful) passive in the game.
Thank you so much, appreciate the detailed response
Tried looking through the spreadsheet guide but it had lots of info and was kinda overwhelming
No problem~
It very much so is, and is easy to get lost in such. You’re always welcome to ask questions in here, and someone will help ya out~

The current banner you want to pull in the following order:

1 copy of thais and Gmurphy -> 1 copy of their wheels -> E2 thais -> E3 Gmurphy -> 1 copy of tulu wheel -> 1 copy of tulu(not a must) -> wheel dupe

There are so crazy broken R wheels that you can use In arc 1 so much esasier for experience!
Gluttony - After a battle you can ditch a orison and instead increase you max hp% two times (always do this)
Frenzy - Allows you to buy Cursed relics in the shop and get some money back after purchasing them (Most cursed relics are very strong)
Emerge - You get an extra refresh in the shop and all of them are free (use it to get better cursed relics)

hey whats a good way to farm keepers exp?
Using stamina and doing stages

you get xp from the daily, the assignments(daily mission where you send chars to collect stuff), spending stamina and from story first clears, and a bit from achievements in pvp/pdive. There's no particularly good way to farm it, just playing will give you xp but it's fairly timegated unless you pay(stamina resets, triple daily premium battle pass, this kind of stuff)

embeds.title: An Exceedingly Brief Arc 1 Morimens Tier List

A very short video retrospective and ranking on my experiences with all of the characters in Morimens Arc 1 "Forgotten Chapters" after over a year and several hundred hours of playing the game. Tier List is ONLY based on Arc 1 performance!!!

01:13 Tier List Parameters & Ranking Sy
